basics.description:
Step 2 of the gh-vmss-img-runners installation flow. This creates the Terraform state backend (resource group + storage account + tfstate container) in your subscription, and grants Storage Blob Data Contributor to the OIDC service principal you created in Step 1.

**Before continuing:** complete Step 1 (`Bootstrap-Entra.ps1`) and copy the **Service Principal Object ID** from its output.

location.label: Region
location.toolTip: Region for the state backend resources.

options.text: If you haven't completed Step 1 yet, cancel this dialog and run Bootstrap-Entra.ps1 first. The Service Principal Object ID from its output is required below.
Paste the 'SP Object ID' value printed by Bootstrap-Entra.ps1 in Step 1. Use the OBJECT ID — not the Client ID.

link.label: What's the difference between Object ID and Client ID?

elements.name: spObjectId
elements.label: Service Principal Object ID
elements.toolTip: Object ID (GUID) of the OIDC service principal from Step 1.

options.text: Default names match exactly what the Terraform backend (`backend.tf`) expects. Override only if you have a naming convention you must follow.

elements.name: resourceGroupName
elements.label: Resource group name
elements.toolTip: Resource group that will hold the state storage account. backend.tf expects 'rg-terraform-state'.

constraints.validationMessage: 1-90 chars: alphanumerics, periods, hyphens, underscores, parentheses.

elements.name: storageAccountName
elements.label: Storage account name (globally unique)
elements.toolTip: 3-24 lowercase alphanumerics, globally unique across Azure. Tip: use something like 'sttfstate' + a short suffix.

elements.name: containerName
elements.label: Blob container name
elements.toolTip: Container that will hold the .tfstate blobs. backend.tf expects 'tfstate'.
